segments.text: Five things about Japan's packaging that separates it from rest of the world.
First thing, milk cartons in Japan have this tiny notch on the top. Blind people can instantly tell real milk from other
drinks just by touch. The government actually researched this problem and solved it with one single cut. Second,
Kikcommen redesigned their soy sauce bottle with a unique spot that gives you perfect drop every time. No more flooding your food. Simple design
change, but it solved a problem that everybody just accepted. Things are going to get interesting now. Third discovery, shampoo bottles have bumps,
conditional bottles are smooths that gives instant recognition in the shower.
Here, Japan created this amazing accessible design which I feel everybody should adapt. Fourth one is the smart packaging that actually changes color
where the food goes wrong even before the printed expiry date. So, no more guessing if your milk has actually turned bad. The package literally shows
you if it is fresh or spoiled in just real time. And finally, the craziest part. If your drink has less than 5%
real juice, no fruit pictures are allowed. 5 to 99% only whole fruits. You can only show sliced food graphics where it contains 100% of the real juice.
